items.title: Down 23% With a 6.7% Yield, Is This High Dividend Stock Too Cheap to Ignore, and Worth Buying in December?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Dow, a commodity chemical giant, has seen its stock price drop 23% year-to-date, pushing its dividend yield up to 6.7%. The article discusses the factors driving the sell-off, including a global slowdown in the industry, and whether Dow is a high-yield dividend stock worth buying for patient investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents a balanced view on Dow, acknowledging the challenges the company is facing due to the global slowdown in the industry, but also suggesting that it could be a good buying opportunity for patient investors who can handle the volatility.
The article mentions that Dow was recently kicked out of the Dow Jones Industrial Average and replaced by Sherwin-Williams, but does not provide any further analysis or sentiment on Sherwin-Williams.
